pattern: The common prefix can only shrink, so compare characters column by column and stop at the first mismatch.
hints: The answer is never longer than the shortest string in the list.
Scan the strings in lockstep, one character position at a time, across every string at once.
For column i, take the character from the first string and require every other string to match it; the moment one fails (or runs out), the prefix is everything before i.

edge_inputs.description: single string is its own prefix
one string is a prefix of the others
strings diverge at the last shared column
an empty string forces an empty prefix

stress.description: many strings sharing a planted prefix of random length
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = string.ascii_lowercase
    plen = rng.randint(0, 8)
    prefix = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(plen))
    n = max(1, size)
    strs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        tail = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 6)))
        strs.append(prefix + tail)
    if rng.random() < 0.3 and strs:
        strs[rng.randrange(len(strs))] = ''
    return [strs]
stress.note: shared prefix is at least `prefix`, possibly extended by chance
